text_excerpt:
EXAMINATION:  PELVIS, NON-OBSTETRIC

INDICATION:  ___ year old woman s/p TLH,BS with extensive lysis of adhesions,
now with vaginal leeding  // evaluate for a cuff hematoma

TECHNIQUE:  Grayscale and Doppler ultrasound images of the pelvis were
obtained with transabdominal approach followed by transvaginal approach.

COMPARISON:  ___ pelvic ultrasound

FINDINGS: 

The uterus is surgically absent.  The ovaries are normal.  No adnexal masses
are identified.  There is no free fluid.  Normal postoperative changes and a
normal-appearing vaginal cuff are noted.  There is no hematoma seen.

IMPRESSION: 

Status post hysterectomy.  Normal postoperative changes and a normal appearing
vaginal cuff are noted.  There is no vaginal cuff hematoma.
